idea.hook: Ever tried calling Mars? Prepare for the ultimate lag!
facts: Light and radio signals travel at 299,792 km/s.
A one-way message to the Moon takes ~1.28 seconds to reach us.
Communicating with a Mars rover can have a 3 to 22 minute one-way delay, depending on orbital positions.
idea.payoff: Every command and conversation across our Solar System isn't real-time—you're always interacting with the past. The universe has a built-in delay!
idea.title: Solar System's Epic Call Lag! 📞⏱️

scenes.text:
Calling Mars?
Prepare for ultimate lag!
scenes.visual: Cosmic phone dialing animation, then a shot of Mars with a 'buffering' icon overlaid.

scenes.text:
Light & Radio Signals travel at:
299,792 km/s
scenes.visual: A line of light streaking across a dark space background, with a speed counter rapidly increasing.
Earth and Moon with a dotted line signal traveling between them. A digital timer counts up to 1.28s.

scenes.text:
Earth to Mars Rover (one-way):
3 to 22 MINUTE Delay!
scenes.visual: Earth and Mars, with a signal traveling. A text bubble over the signal path displays '3 min' quickly morphing to '22 min' as if planets' positions change. A small rover icon on Mars.

scenes.text:
Every command, every message...
is interacting with the PAST.
The universe has a built-in delay!
scenes.visual: A conceptual animation of our solar system with light beams connecting planets. A large, stylized 'TIME DELAY' text overlay grows and pulses.
